sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Supermicro Expands GPU Solutions Portfolio with Deskside Liquid-Cooled AI Development Platform, Powered by NVIDIA
feed.summary: Supermicro has launched a new liquid-cooled AI development platform, the SYS-751GE-TNRT-NV1, designed for deskside use. This platform features four NVIDIA A100 Tensor Core GPUs and two 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable CPUs, providing a powerful, quiet, and energy-efficient solution for AI/HPC workloads. It also includes a three-year subscription to NVIDIA AI Enterprise, simplifying software setup for developers.

feed.title: FWD Top Holdings List & Exposure (AB Disruptors ETF)
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed list of the top holdings and exposure for the AB Disruptors ETF (FWD). The ETF's top 25 holdings are listed by weight, comprising 38.47% of the total fund, and include major companies like Broadcom and NVIDIA. The article also touches on FWD's investment strategy, focusing on theme strategies within the Global region.

feed.title: Taiwan PCB makers moving production to Southeast Asia
feed.summary: Taiwanese Printed Circuit Board (PCB) manufacturers are relocating production from China to Southeast Asian countries like Thailand, Malaysia, and Vietnam. This move is driven by factors such as investment incentives and lower manufacturing costs in these regions, despite challenges including wastewater management and anti-Chinese sentiment in some areas. Companies like Unimicron, Kinsus Interconnect Technology, and Tripod Technology are establishing or expanding facilities, capitalizing on the growing semiconductor supply chain in Malaysia and Vietnam.

feed.title: Intel co-founder and philanthropist Gordon Moore has died at 94
feed.summary: Gordon Moore, the visionary co-founder of Intel and profound philanthropist, has passed away at the age of 94. He was widely recognized for his groundbreaking "Moore's Law," which accurately predicted the exponential growth of computing power, significantly influencing the trajectory of the digital age. Beyond his technological contributions, Moore, alongside his wife, established a foundation that has donated over $5.1 billion to environmental conservation, science, and patient care.

feed.title: The Strange Case of an Israeli Computer Lab Flagged by the U.S.
feed.summary: The U.S. Commerce Department added Israeli company CNG Labs to its "unverified list," restricting its ability to do business with American firms, following a prior Israeli confiscation of CNG's processors. The Commerce Department's action marks an unusual move against an Israeli company and is a "red flag" warning, not a sanction or blacklist. CNG Labs, which claims to be a computer store, has disputed the dual-use classification of its older model processors, arguing they are only for laptops.

feed.title: AutoCAD for Mac 2024: New Collaboration Enhancements and Improved Performance With M-Series Chips
feed.summary: AutoCAD for Mac 2024 and AutoCAD LT for Mac 2024 have been released, introducing significant performance enhancements, particularly for Apple Silicon M-series chips, which can double performance compared to the previous version. The new release focuses on improving collaboration and automation with features like Markup Import and Markup Assist, Smart Blocks for automatic block placement, and updates to the Trace environment. Additionally, it includes 3D Color Background customization, 3D Connexion device support, and new language support for Italian and Spanish.

feed.title: ECE Expeditions rides to the Lone Star State for a tour of engineering companies
feed.summary: ECE Expeditions took fourteen students to Texas for a firsthand look at various engineering companies in Houston and Austin, including Hewlett Packard Enterprise, NASA, Ambiq, Dell Technologies, Torc Robotics, and NI. Students networked with Michigan alums, explored technology demos, and toured labs, gaining valuable insights into careers in electrical and computer engineering. The trip aimed to provide students with motivation and clarity on their future career paths in the engineering industry.

feed.title: Micron Could Report Largest Ever Loss in Q2
feed.summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) is projected to report its largest-ever loss in the second fiscal quarter of 2023, with an estimated net loss of nearly $1.2 billion and EPS of -$1.06, a significant drop from the prior year. This financial struggle is attributed to weak consumer demand, high inflation, inventory issues, and industry-wide supply constraints. Analysts predict negative gross margins for the first time in 15 years, with an expected inventory write-down in the range of a billion dollars.

feed.title: Intel’s stock set for best month in 22 years as execs lay out plans to take on AMD’s rise in servers
feed.summary: Intel's stock experienced its best month since 2001, with shares jumping 7.6% on Wednesday after an investor presentation detailed the company's strategy to regain market share in data-center chips. The company aims to reverse its manufacturing decline and compete more effectively against AMD. This surge pushed Intel's March gain to 26.4%.

feed.title: 3 Best Desktop Computers for Business from HP India
feed.summary: This article identifies and reviews the three best HP desktop computers for business use: the HP Workstation Z4 G4 Tower, the HP Z1 G9 Tower Desktop PC, and the HP Z2 Mini G9 Workstation Wolf Pro Security Edition. It outlines key features to consider when purchasing a business desktop, including form factor, processor, graphics, memory, storage, and security. The article emphasizes that the ideal business computer depends on specific needs like raw processing power, upgradability, or a compact design.

feed.title: Beijing’s Micron Probe Spurs $12 Billion Rally in China Chip Stocks
feed.summary: Chinese chip stocks experienced a significant rally, collectively adding over $14 billion in market value, following Beijing's probe into Micron Technology Inc. This surge reflects optimism that domestic firms will benefit from China's increased focus on self-reliance in the semiconductor industry. Companies like Ingenic Semiconductor and Advanced Micro-Fabrication Equipment saw substantial gains.

feed.title: XBIL Stock Price | US Treasury 6 Month Bill ETF
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information about the US Treasury 6 Month Bill ETF (XBIL), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NASDAQ), key statistics, dividend yield, and expense ratio. It also features recent news and analysis related to XBIL and US Treasury bills, alongside technical analysis ratings and performance data. The ETF is managed by F/m Investments LLC and aims to track the performance of the Bloomberg US Treasury Bellwether 6M Total Return USD Unhedged Index.

feed.title: LG is creating new 4K and Ultrawide OLED displays with refresh rates of 240 Hz
feed.summary: LG is developing new 4K and Ultrawide OLED displays with a 240 Hz refresh rate, expected to launch in 2024. These displays will come in various sizes, including 27-inch, 31.5-inch, and 42-inch 4K models, and 34-inch, 39-inch, and 45-inch ultrawide options. Some models will also feature dual refresh-rate modes, offering flexibility between resolution and performance for gamers.

feed.title: Samsung Electronics and AMD Extend Strategic IP Licensing Agreement to Bring AMD Radeon™ Graphics to Future Mobile Platforms
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics and AMD have announced a multi-year extension of their strategic IP licensing agreement. This collaboration will integrate multiple generations of high-performance, ultra-low-power AMD Radeon graphics solutions into an expanded portfolio of Samsung Exynos SoCs, bringing console-level graphics and optimized power consumption to mobile devices. This builds upon their initial 2019 partnership which resulted in the Samsung Xclipse mobile GPU, featuring industry-first mobile ray tracing.

feed.title: AMD Is Space Certified As Xilinx Computers Make It To NASA’s Rocket & Ship
feed.summary: AMD announced that its subsidiary Xilinx's field-programmable gate arrays (FPGAs) are integral to NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft, both central to the Artemis program aimed at returning humans to the Moon. These FPGAs are radiation-hardened, crucial for operating in the harsh space environment, and highlight AMD's diversification into the FPGA sector following its acquisition of Xilinx. The announcement coincided with NASA's crew selection for the Artemis 2 mission, underscoring the critical role of AMD's technology in future lunar explorations.

feed.title: ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed financial overview of the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW), including its current stock price, key statistics, top holdings, dividend data, and performance. As of the latest N-PORT filing on January 31, 2026, the ETF holds 45 stocks with Tesla, Advanced Micro Devices, and ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF as its largest positions. The fund's financial strength and growth ranks are strong, though its GF Value rank is moderate.

feed.title: DFEM Stock Price | Dimensional Emerging Markets Core Equity 2 ETF
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information on the Dimensional Emerging Markets Core Equity 2 ETF (DFEM), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NYSE), and key statistics like expense ratio, dividend yield, and inception date. It also lists its top holdings, sector allocation, and technical analysis indicators, helping investors assess its potential.

feed.title: Empowering The Industry with Open System Firmware – AMD openSIL
feed.summary: AMD has introduced openSIL, an open-source silicon initialization library, to address challenges in firmware such as security vulnerabilities and limited transparency. This initiative aims to improve platform security, functionality, and traceability by opening up firmware architecture and development to the open-source community. AMD is collaborating with several organizations to develop and demonstrate openSIL, which reorganizes its existing AGESA framework into a more flexible and scalable solution for various host firmware types.

feed.title: 3 Best HP Pavilion Laptops For Entertainment | HP® Tech Takes
feed.summary: This article outlines the key features to consider when choosing an entertainment laptop, focusing on display size, audio, storage, memory, and graphics. It then presents three top HP Pavilion laptop models—the Pavilion x360, Pavilion 15, and Pavilion Gaming laptop—highlighting their specifications and suitability for various entertainment needs. The guide aims to help users find a budget-friendly and high-performance device for gaming, streaming, or general multimedia consumption.

feed.title: AMD Bolsters Embedded Portfolio with New Ryzen Embedded 5000 Series Processors for Networking Solutions
feed.summary: AMD has announced the availability of its new Ryzen Embedded 5000 Series processors, designed for power-efficient, "always-on" networking firewalls, network-attached storage, and other security applications. These "Zen 3"-based processors offer scalable performance with up to 16 cores, ECC-supported memory, and PCIe Gen4 connectivity, ideal for demanding enterprise reliability requirements in space- and power-constrained environments. The new series expands AMD's embedded portfolio, bridging the gap between its low-power Ryzen Embedded and high-performance EPYC Embedded families.

feed.title: Analog Devices Appoints Alan Lee as Chief Technology Officer
feed.summary: Analog Devices, Inc. (ADI) has appointed Alan Lee as its new Chief Technology Officer (CTO). In this role, Lee will lead the identification and advancement of next-generation technologies for the semiconductor industry, collaborating with customers, universities, and partners. He brings extensive experience from his previous role as Head of Research and Advanced Development at AMD and will report directly to ADI CEO and Chair Vincent Roche.

feed.title: Analog Devices names former AMD exec Alan Lee as CTO
feed.summary: Analog Devices has appointed Alan Lee, formerly head of research and advanced development at AMD, as its new Chief Technology Officer. In this role, Lee will focus on identifying and advancing next-generation technologies to disrupt and shape the semiconductor industry. He will report directly to Analog Devices' CEO Vincent Roche and brings extensive experience in hardware, software, AI, and high-performance computing from his tenure at AMD, Intel, and IBM.

feed.title: Analog Devices appoints former AMD executive Alan Lee as CTO
feed.summary: Analog Devices has appointed Alan Lee, former head of research and advanced development at AMD, as its new Chief Technology Officer (CTO). In this role, Lee will focus on next-generation semiconductor technologies and collaborate with various partners to drive innovation. He will report directly to ADI's CEO and is expected to accelerate the company's leadership in areas such as analog, mixed-signal, power, software, and AI capabilities.

feed.title: Lam Research has closed its U.S. facilities for the week amid the chip downturn
feed.summary: Lam Research has temporarily closed its U.S. facilities for an additional week this year due to a downturn in the semiconductor industry and decreased demand. This marks a third week of closure, beyond the typical two, and follows previous announcements of job cuts by the company.

feed.title: Cognitive Computing Brings Human Touch
feed.summary: Cognitive computing, particularly neuromorphic computing, is poised to transform artificial intelligence by enabling systems to "think" like humans, learn from data, and interact naturally. This technology, which uses artificial neurons to mimic the human brain, could power various applications from autonomous vehicles to smart devices with greater efficiency. While companies developing this technology present investment opportunities, the article suggests that adopting companies leveraging it for increased productivity and growth may offer even greater upside, while also highlighting the ethical considerations and risks associated with advanced AI systems.

feed.title: Intel says margins will recover in second half of 2023, shares rise
feed.summary: Intel announced that its gross margins are expected to improve in the second half of 2023, after hitting historic lows in the first half of the year. The chipmaker also reported shipping its delayed Sapphire Rapids data center chip in volume and observed signs of stability in the PC market, leading to a 4% rise in its shares in after-hours trading. Despite forecasting adjusted losses for the second quarter, the company's Q1 revenue slightly exceeded estimates, and CEO Pat Gelsinger discussed the ongoing Tower Semiconductor acquisition with Chinese officials.

feed.title: Wilmington's Analog Devices Names Alan Lee Chief Technology Officer
feed.summary: Analog Devices, Inc. (ADI), a semiconductor leader based in Wilmington, MA, has appointed Alan Lee as its new chief technology officer. Lee, previously head of research and advanced development at AMD, will focus on advancing next-generation analog, mixed-signal, power, software, and AI capabilities for ADI. His role will involve collaborating with customers, universities, and partners to develop novel technologies and support their market entry.

feed.title: Semiconductor supplier secures incentives in Round Rock for $185M, 50-job expansion
feed.summary: Toppan Photomasks, a semiconductor industry supplier, has secured incentives for a $185 million expansion in Round Rock, Texas. This project is expected to create 50 new jobs at its existing site. The company counts major players like Samsung, Texas Instruments, and AMD among its clients.

feed.title: IBM to Replace 7,800 Jobs Using Automation and AI
feed.summary: IBM plans to replace approximately 7,800 jobs over the next five years using automation and AI technology. CEO Arvind Krishna stated that this would primarily impact non-customer-facing roles, with about 30% of back-office positions expected to be replaced. The company will also slow or suspend hiring in departments like human resources, while still looking for engineering and customer-facing talent.

feed.title: Opinion: AMD stock is sliding after earnings, and these numbers show why
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock slid significantly after the company reported a first-quarter net loss of $139 million and a revenue decline to $5.3 billion. This downturn was largely attributed to substantial drops in PC sales and gaming, exacerbated by a big decline in profit for its key data-center group and an unoptimistic forecast. The chip maker's overall margin was severely impacted, leading to a nearly 7% drop in its shares in after-hours trading.

feed.title: AMD to Make 'New' 2019-Era 3000G APUs to Sell Old Motherboards
feed.summary: AMD is reportedly resuming production of its 2019-era 12nm Zen+ Ryzen 3000G APUs, specifically the Ryzen 3 3200G and Ryzen 5 3400G, with plans to produce 30,000 new chips. This move is believed to be an effort to stimulate sales of older AM4 motherboards, particularly in the Chinese market. The article notes that price adjustments will be necessary for these dated APUs to be competitive against newer, similarly priced options like the Ryzen 5 5600G.

feed.title: Growing chip supply helps Baxter slash backlog
feed.summary: Baxter International significantly reduced its backlog for Front Line Care products by half in Q1, thanks to an improved supply of electromechanical components, including semiconductor chips. This improved availability has allowed the healthcare company to reduce spot purchasing, lower costs, and enhance operational predictability, though an elevated backlog still remains which the company aims to address throughout the year. Despite facing continued pressure from rising supply chain costs from suppliers, Baxter expresses cautious optimism about turning the corner on component supply.

feed.title: Intel layoffs 2023: Targeting $10B in annual savings
feed.summary: Intel is reportedly planning significant layoffs, potentially affecting up to 20% of employees in its client computing and data center divisions, as part of a strategy to achieve $10 billion in annual savings by 2026. These workforce reductions come amidst a challenging macroeconomic environment, slowed chip demand, and increasing competition from rivals like AMD. Despite a record first-quarter loss, Intel anticipates chip demand recovery in the second half of 2023 and is focusing on advanced manufacturing processes to regain market share.

feed.title: Qualcomm buys vehicle chip startup AutoTalks in reported $350M+ deal
feed.summary: Qualcomm has acquired AutoTalks Ltd., an Israeli chip startup specializing in V2X (vehicle-to-everything) communication technology for enhanced driver safety. The deal, reportedly valued between $350 million and $400 million, will integrate AutoTalks' chips, which support both C-V2X and DSRC standards, into Qualcomm's Snapdragon Digital Chassis product portfolio. This acquisition is expected to accelerate the adoption of V2X technology and bolster Qualcomm's offerings in the automotive sector, following its 2021 acquisition of Arriver.

feed.title: Notable Wins
feed.summary: The I/O Fund has achieved significant returns since its inception, outperforming major indices with a cumulative return of 326%. In 2025 alone, the fund returned 37%, positioning it as a top-performing hedge fund. The article details specific successful investments in companies like Bloom Energy, Astera Labs, Nvidia, and various cryptocurrencies, highlighting the fund's strategic allocation to AI and early insights into market trends.

feed.title: Vehicle network startup Ethernovia raises $64 million
feed.summary: Ethernovia Inc., a startup focused on vehicle networks, has successfully raised $64 million in Series A funding. The investment round saw participation from notable investors including Porsche Automobile Holding SE, Qualcomm Ventures, and AMD Ventures. The company aims to develop cutting-edge network architecture, high-performance computing, and high-bandwidth communications for the next generation of software-defined vehicles, addressing the increasing data transmission demands in the automotive industry.

feed.title: Implications and Updates From China’s National People’s Conference
feed.summary: China's annual "Two Sessions" parliamentary meeting revealed strategic policies for economic growth, trade, and technology, targeting a 5% GDP growth in 2023 driven by domestic consumption. The conference highlighted key implications for sectors like EVs, clean energy, cloud computing, biotechnology, and semiconductors, emphasizing self-reliance and technological innovation. Government restructuring and increased focus on digital infrastructure and R&D are expected to significantly impact various industries and investment themes in China.

feed.title: Canaan unveils immersion-cooled Avalon Made A1366I Bitcoin miner
feed.summary: Canaan has launched its new immersion-cooled Avalon Made A1366I Bitcoin miner, featuring a hashrate of 165 TH/s and an efficiency of 30 J/TH, which offers benefits like safer overclocking and extended lifespan. This release follows their earlier 13-series models and comes amidst competitive market conditions and fluctuating stock prices for the company, which was the first mining equipment manufacturer to go public.

feed.title: Supermicro Launches Industry's First NVIDIA HGX H100 8 and 4-GPU H100 Servers with Liquid Cooling -- Reduces Data Center Power Costs by Up to 40%
feed.summary: Supermicro has launched the industry's first NVIDIA HGX H100 8 and 4-GPU servers featuring liquid cooling, designed to significantly reduce data center power costs by up to 40%. These new rack-scale solutions accelerate deployment and increase performance for AI and HPC workloads, while also lowering operating expenses and improving Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) by up to 86% in direct cooling costs. The systems offer up to 30x performance improvement over previous generations for large transformer models and boast advanced features like PCIe 5.0 and NVIDA H100 SXM5 Tensor Core GPUs.

feed.title: Applied Materials plans $4B Silicon Valley R&D facility
feed.summary: Applied Materials announced plans to invest up to $4 billion in a new Silicon Valley research and development facility. This "EPIC Center" will focus on advancing semiconductor manufacturing and is expected to house over $25 billion in research work within its first decade. The investment is contingent on receiving funding from the CHIPS and Science Act, aiming to accelerate the industry's ability to bring new technologies to market.

feed.title: Supermicro Launches Industry's First NVIDIA HGX H100 8 and 4-GPU H100 Servers with Liquid Cooling -- Reduces Data Center Power Costs by Up to 40%
feed.summary: Supermicro has introduced the industry's first NVIDIA HGX H100 8 and 4-GPU servers featuring liquid cooling, designed to significantly reduce data center power costs by up to 40%. These new rack-scale solutions accelerate deployment, enhance performance, and lower operating expenses, also offering an estimated 86% reduction in direct cooling costs compared to air-cooled data centers. The servers support high-performance AI workloads with NVIDIA HGX H100 Tensor Core GPUs and are integrated with advanced liquid cooling systems to optimize Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) and Total Cost to Environment (TCE).

feed.title: Applied Materials Launches Multibillion-Dollar R&D Platform in Silicon Valley to Accelerate Semiconductor Innovation
feed.summary: Applied Materials, Inc. announced a multibillion-dollar investment to build the Equipment and Process Innovation and Commercialization (EPIC) Center in Silicon Valley. This facility will be the world’s largest and most advanced for collaborative semiconductor process technology and manufacturing equipment R&D, aiming to accelerate innovation and reduce the time to commercialization for new technologies. The center will provide dedicated space for chipmakers and university researchers, fostering collaboration and addressing critical industry challenges like talent shortages and R&D costs.

feed.title: Applied Materials plans $4B Silicon Valley chip research facility
feed.summary: Applied Materials plans to build a $4 billion chip research facility in Sunnyvale, California, part of a larger potential investment of up to $25 billion over 10 years, contingent on support from the CHIPS Act. This facility is expected to create 1,500 construction jobs and aims to accelerate semiconductor technology and manufacturing equipment research and development. The project signifies the CHIPS Act's success in attracting significant private investment back to the U.S. semiconductor industry.

feed.title: Applied Materials plans to build US$4bn R&D center
feed.summary: Applied Materials plans to invest up to US$4 billion to construct a new Research and Development (R&D) center in Silicon Valley, aiming to accelerate semiconductor manufacturing innovation. This "EPIC Center" would be the largest R&D facility in the semiconductor industry and is envisioned to speed up the transition of academic research to factory production, while also potentially leveraging funding from the US CHIPS and Science Act. The project seeks to address technical challenges and attract talent crucial for the industry's growth.

feed.title: Latest Top500 List Highlights World's Fastest and Most Energy Efficient Supercomputers Powered by AMD
feed.summary: AMD announced its continued leadership in high-performance computing (HPC), with its EPYC processors and Instinct accelerators powering many of the world's fastest and most energy-efficient supercomputers. The Frontier supercomputer, equipped with AMD technology, remains the fastest globally and has entered full user operations for scientific research. AMD's focus extends to supporting AI applications within HPC through its hardware and the open ROCm software platform, highlighting systems like LUMI for large language model training.

feed.title: New AMD Ryzen™ 7020 C-Series Processors Bring Fast Performance, Longer Battery Life, and Advanced RDNA™ 2 Graphics to Chromebook Users
feed.summary: AMD has announced its new Ryzen and Athlon 7020 C-Series processors, designed to enhance Chromebook performance, battery life, and graphics capabilities. These new processors, built on 6nm technology with "Zen 2" architecture and RDNA 2 integrated graphics, promise faster and more efficient operation for productivity, learning, and streaming. Chromebooks from ASUS and Dell featuring these processors are expected to be available in Q2 2023.

feed.title: SPRX Top Holdings List & Exposure (Spear Alpha ETF)
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed list of the top holdings and exposure for the Spear Alpha ETF (SPRX). It identifies Coherent, AST SpaceMobile, and KLA as the top three holdings, which collectively represent a significant portion of the fund's total assets. The article also breaks down SPRX's geographic, currency, sector, industry, and sub-industry exposures, highlighting a strong concentration in the Technology sector and the Semiconductors & Semiconductor Equipment industry.

feed.title: Fidelity Metaverse ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:FMET)
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of the Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET), including its price, holdings, and recent news. FMET tracks a market cap-weighted index of equities related to the metaverse, launched on April 19, 2022, and is managed by Fidelity. The ETF has seen a significant increase in share price since the beginning of 2025 and lists its top institutional shareholders and key holdings.

feed.title: Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:SOXQ)
feed.summary: The Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) tracks a market-cap-weighted index of 30 U.S.-listed semiconductor companies, launched in June 2021. As of March 2026, SOXQ was trading at $64.09, showing a 14.9% increase year-to-date, with key holdings including NVIDIA, Broadcom, and Advanced Micro Devices. The ETF manages over $1 billion in assets, driven partly by significant investments in AI chip stocks.

feed.title: SOXQ Top Holdings List & Exposure (Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF)
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed list of the top holdings and exposure for the Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ). It breaks down the fund's investments by company, geographic region, currency, sector, and industry. The top holdings include NVIDIA, Broadcom, and Advanced Micro Devices, with a significant allocation to the United States and the technology sector, specifically semiconductors.

feed.title: Applied Materials' Big Bet: A ‘Unique’ $4B Chip R&D Center
feed.summary: Applied Materials is investing up to $4 billion to build the "EPIC Center," a 180,000-square-foot semiconductor R&D facility in Silicon Valley. This unique center will bring together chipmakers, academics, and ecosystem partners to accelerate manufacturing innovation, reduce costs, and shorten product development timelines, especially crucial in the complex 'angstrom era' of chipmaking. The investment's final size is contingent on subsidies from the U.S. government’s CHIPS and Science Act.

feed.title: Intel's Arc A750 graphics card is only $200 at Newegg
feed.summary: Intel's Arc A750 graphics card is currently available for $200 on Newegg, a $50 discount, making it an excellent option for affordable 1080p gaming. This deal is significant as GPU prices are decreasing due to increased competition from Intel's Arc series. The card performs best with motherboards supporting Resizeable BAR, especially for DirectX 12 titles.

feed.title: Nvidia stock price is surging thanks to ChatGPT and an expected boom in generative AI
feed.summary: Nvidia's stock price surged over 26% after forecasting Q2 2024 revenue of $11 billion, significantly above Wall Street estimates, due to the increasing demand for its powerful GPU chips needed for generative AI technologies like ChatGPT. Despite a slight decrease in Q1 2024 revenue compared to the previous year, the company's strong future outlook based on the generative AI boom is driving investor confidence, also positively impacting other chipmakers like AMD. Nvidia's CEO stated that the company is greatly increasing its supply to meet the surging demand for its data center products.

feed.title: ETF of the Week: Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP)
feed.summary: VettaFi’s Tom Lydon discusses the Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP) which offers exposure to top holdings favored by hedge funds without an overweight toward any single stock. GVIP provides diversification across various companies, including some mid-cap and international stocks, making it a less volatile option for investors seeking innovative companies. It provides a consensus of top companies chosen by leading hedge fund managers, appealing to investors looking for strong, well-vetted companies.

feed.title: ETF of the Week: Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP)
feed.summary: The Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP) is highlighted as the ETF of the Week. GVIP provides exposure to top stock picks from hedge funds, focusing on a consensus of leading managers rather than an overweighting of individual stocks. The ETF diversifies holdings across large-cap, mid-cap, and international companies, offering a less volatile way for investors to participate in innovative companies.

feed.title: Markets Brief: Why Vanguard Sees a Brighter Outlook for Investors’ Portfolios
feed.summary: Vanguard Group forecasts a brighter outlook for investors, primarily due to higher bond yields that now offer meaningful contributions to portfolios. While stock valuations remain stretched in the U.S., increasing interest rates have made fixed-income investments more attractive, particularly government bonds. Vanguard's long-term forecasts suggest improved returns for both bonds and stocks compared to recent years, highlighting a more balanced investment landscape.

feed.title: NVIDIA Announces DGX GH200 AI Supercomputer
feed.summary: NVIDIA has unveiled the DGX GH200 AI supercomputer, designed to handle massive models for generative AI, recommender systems, and data analytics. This new system connects 256 Grace Hopper Superchips to function as a single 1-exaflop, 144TB GPU, offering significantly more memory than its predecessor. Companies like Google Cloud, Meta, and Microsoft are among the first to explore its capabilities, and NVIDIA is also building its own Helios supercomputer based on this design.

feed.title: Microsoft shares fix for cameras not working on Surface laptops
feed.summary: Microsoft has released a temporary fix for an issue preventing built-in cameras from working on some ARM-based Surface devices, including the Surface Pro X. The problem, caused by a buggy driver, affects devices running Windows 10 22H2 or Windows 11 21H2/22H2 with specific Qualcomm processors. While a troubleshooter is being deployed automatically, users can apply a manual registry edit or await a permanent driver update.

feed.title: Student profile: Gaining skills to work in signal integrity | Electrical, Computer & Energy Engineering
feed.summary: Vivek Kamble, a professional master's student in high-speed digital engineering at CU Boulder, shares his journey from teaching in India to pursuing advanced studies in signal integrity. He highlights the practical curriculum and industry expert-taught courses that prepared him for multiple internship offers, including one with Advanced Micro Devices Inc. His advice emphasizes the high demand for signal integrity and high-speed digital engineers globally.

feed.title: Here’s how Applied Materials manages supply chain and semiconductor research
feed.summary: Applied Materials plans to invest US$4 billion over seven years into a collaborative R&D facility in Silicon Valley, the Equipment and Process Innovation and Commercialization (EPIC) Center, to accelerate semiconductor technology development. The company emphasizes the critical role of R&D and collaboration in the semiconductor industry and discusses global supply chain changes and its investment strategy in the US and Singapore. Applied Materials also outlines its Singapore 2030 plan, focusing on manufacturing capacity expansion, deepening R&D, strengthening partnerships, and talent development, with Singapore serving as its sole Center of Excellence for advanced packaging.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries and STMicroelectronics finalize agreement for new 300mm semiconductor manufacturing facility in France
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries and STMicroelectronics have finalized an agreement to establish a new 300mm semiconductor manufacturing facility in Crolles, France, first announced in July 2022. This joint venture, supported by significant financial aid from the French government and European Commission, aims to boost semiconductor production capacity in Europe. The facility represents a projected cost of 7.5 billion euro and will focus on technologies for key markets like automotive, industrial, IoT, and communication infrastructure.

feed.title: Samsung Electronics makes 17-fold gains from investment in ASML
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics has seen its initial investment in ASML Holding N.V. grow 17-fold, reaching $4.56 billion, despite reducing its stake to 1.6%. The article highlights how major chipmakers like Samsung, Intel, and TSMC invested in ASML over a decade ago when the company needed funding for R&D. The gains underscore the critical importance of ASML's EUV scanners in the semiconductor industry, with Samsung actively securing these machines to compete with market leader TSMC.

feed.title: Intel Reveals Fastest Arc Pro Workstation GPUs Yet
feed.summary: Intel has launched its fastest Arc Pro workstation GPUs to date, the A60 for desktops and A60M for laptops, significantly increasing specifications over previous models. These new GPUs are designed for applications like CAD, AI inferencing, and media processing, aiming to challenge the market dominance of Nvidia and AMD. While Intel hopes to attract workstation users with improved performance and quarterly driver updates, some partners note a lack of demand and suggest Intel needs a more comprehensive solution to compete effectively.

feed.title: Applied Materials Announces $4 Billion Investment In EPIC Center For R&D Collaboration
feed.summary: Applied Materials is investing $4 billion into its new Equipment and Process Innovation and Commercialization (EPIC) Center, a collaborative R&D project designed to accelerate semiconductor production techniques. This initiative aims to reduce the time from foundational research to mass commercialization by 30% through overlapping R&D phases and fostering direct collaboration between universities, equipment makers, and chip companies. The EPIC Center will strengthen Applied Materials' position in the industry, benefit chip makers with faster product roadmaps, and provide invaluable learning opportunities for university students.

feed.title: RSPT Top Holdings List & Exposure (Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Technology ETF)
feed.summary: This article details the top holdings, geographic exposure, currency exposure, and sector/industry exposure of the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Technology ETF (RSPT). Key holdings include Intel, Advanced Micro Devices, and Micron Technology, with the fund primarily invested in U.S. information technology companies. The article provides a comprehensive breakdown of where RSPT allocates its investments.

feed.title: Supermicro Expands AMD Product Lines with New Servers and New Processors Optimized for Cloud Native Infrastructure and High-Performance Technical Computing
feed.summary: Supermicro is expanding its AMD product lines with new servers and processors optimized for cloud-native infrastructure and high-performance technical computing. The new systems support 4th Gen AMD EPYC™ processors, including those with "Zen 4c" architecture and AMD 3D V-Cache™ technology, offering enhanced density, energy efficiency, and accelerated performance for various workloads. This expansion aims to meet customer requirements for resource-saving, application-optimized servers with rack scale integration.

feed.title: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Announces OCI Compute Instances Based on New 4th Generation AMD EPYC Processors
feed.summary: Oracle announced plans to make new OCI Compute E5 instances available, featuring 4th generation AMD EPYC™ processors, to help organizations balance price and performance and reduce cloud costs. These flexible instances will offer more CPU cores, better performance, and higher storage capacity than prior generations, with customers already saving $40 million annually by using OCI's flexible compute with AMD processors. The new instances are designed for a wide range of workloads, supporting applications from Oracle, Microsoft, SAP, and others, and will be generally available in the second half of 2023.

feed.title: AMD launches new data-center AI chips, software to go up against Nvidia and Intel
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) unveiled new data-center AI chips and software, including the Instinct MI300X accelerator and MI300A, to compete with Nvidia Corp. and Intel Corp. Despite these announcements at its Data Center & AI Technology Premiere event, AMD's stock declined, finishing down 3.6%. The new products are specifically aimed at handling generative-AI workloads.

feed.title: AMD launches new Instinct MI300X AI chip to take on Nvidia’s GPUs
feed.summary: AMD has launched its new Instinct MI300X AI accelerator chip, designed to compete with Nvidia's GPUs in the generative AI market. The company also introduced its Infinity Architecture Platform, powered by eight MI300X chips, and its ROCm software ecosystem. Despite these announcements, AMD's stock fell due to a lack of major customer endorsements for the new chips and details on pricing.

feed.title: AMD's AI chip challenge to Nvidia remains uphill fight with no big customers named
feed.summary: AMD unveiled new details about its MI300X artificial intelligence chip, designed to challenge Nvidia's market dominance, but did not announce any major customers for the product. While the chip offers significant memory capacity aimed at cost reduction for AI services, the lack of customer commitments disappointed Wall Street, causing AMD's stock to fall. Analysts noted that Nvidia's strong market position is reinforced by its software ecosystem, which AMD is attempting to counter with its Rocm platform.

feed.title: AMD is chasing down Nvidia in AI, but one analyst worries the company is ‘somewhat late’
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) is pursuing the lucrative AI data-center market, estimated to be worth $150 billion, following recent product announcements. While many analysts praised AMD's efforts, Bernstein analyst Stacy Rasgon expressed concerns that AMD is "somewhat late" to the AI race compared to rival Nvidia Corp. Rasgon noted that AMD's AI discussion offered limited "hard numbers" and a timeline that seemed delayed relative to the competition.

feed.title: The A.I. Race Is Now Between Two Chip Giants Led by Taiwanese American CEOs
feed.summary: The generative A.I. boom has intensified competition between semiconductor giants Nvidia and AMD, who are racing to produce advanced A.I. chips. AMD recently announced the MI300X, a direct competitor to Nvidia's H100, aiming to challenge Nvidia's dominant market share in the A.I. chip sector. Both companies are led by Taiwanese American CEOs, Jensen Huang of Nvidia and Lisa Su of AMD, who share similar backgrounds and have played pivotal roles in their companies' success in the semiconductor industry.

feed.title: Fidelity Metaverse ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (FMET)
feed.summary: The Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" with an average price target equal to its current share price of $36.66. The rating is based on 613 analyst ratings covering 20 companies (69.5% of the portfolio) over the past year. The ETF's top holdings include major tech companies like AMD, Alphabet, Apple, and NVIDIA.

feed.title: Spear Alpha ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (SPRX)
feed.summary: The Spear Alpha ETF (SPRX) currently holds an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" based on 557 analyst ratings covering 25 companies within its portfolio. These companies represent 99.4% of the ETF's holdings. The aggregate price target for SPRX is $37.53, with individual holdings showing possible upsides ranging from -26.1% to 75.3%.
